title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
coverScene: Sherlock Holmes sitting in his armchair, surrounded by books and papers, with a hint of a London street scene outside the window.

sentences.text: It was a cold winter evening when I visited my old friend Sherlock Holmes.
He was living in our old place in Baker Street, which was always full of books and papers.
As I walked up the stairs, I felt a sense of excitement.
The wind was blowing hard outside, and the streets were covered in white snow.
When I entered the room, I saw Holmes sitting in his chair.
He looked very thin and tired, but his eyes were still bright.
A small fire was burning in the room, which made the place feel warm and comfortable.
Holmes welcomed me with a smile.
He pointed to an empty chair next to the fire.
If I had not visited him that night, I would have missed a very interesting discussion.
I sat down and began to tell him about my own life.
I told him about my work as a doctor, which kept me very busy.
My family was doing well, and my life was quiet and happy.
Holmes listened to me carefully, though he did not say much at first.
He was holding a pipe in his hand, which was not burning.
I could see that his mind was active, even if his body was tired.
He had been working on some difficult problems, which had taken all his energy.
It was clear that he needed a new challenge to make him feel alive again.
While we were talking, a letter was brought to the room by a servant.
The letter had been left at the door just a few minutes before.
Holmes opened the envelope and read the note inside with great interest.
He did not say anything for a moment, but his face showed that it was important.
Then he handed the note to me so that I could read it too.
The note was written on thick paper, which looked very expensive.
It said that a gentleman would visit Holmes at a quarter to eight that night.
The writer did not give his name or explain what he wanted.
However, the note warned Holmes to be careful and to expect something important.
We began to talk about who this visitor might be.
If the paper had been cheap, we might have thought it was a joke.
But the paper was of very high quality, which suggested the writer was a rich man.
"This is a very interesting matter," Holmes said, looking down at the floor.
"Why would a gentleman write a note instead of coming directly?"
He believed that the visitor was facing a very serious problem.
If the problem were simple, he would not have asked for help in this way.
We waited in silence as the clock on the wall moved toward the hour.
My heart was beating fast because I wanted to know what would happen next.
At exactly a quarter to eight, we heard the sound of wheels outside.
A vehicle had stopped in front of our house in Baker Street.
A moment later, heavy steps were heard on the stairs.
There was a knock on the door, and Holmes called out for the visitor to enter.
The door opened, and a tall, thin man stepped into the room.
He was wearing a dark coat that covered him from head to foot.
But what was most unusual was that he wore something over his face.
It was a black mask, which hid his eyes and the upper part of his face.
This made him look very strange, and I wondered why he wanted to hide his identity.
The gentleman stood by the door for a moment without saying a word.
He looked at me and then at Holmes, as if he were trying to decide something.
"Should I speak before your friend?" he asked in a low voice.
Holmes replied that I was a trusted friend who could keep a secret.
If I had not been there, the visitor might have spoken more freely at first.
But because Holmes trusted me, the gentleman decided to stay and explain his problem.
He sat down in the chair that I had offered him.
He spoke slowly, and his voice was deep and full of worry.
He told us that his problem was so serious that it could ruin his life.
The visitor explained that he was a person of very high position in his country.
If his secret were discovered, it would cause a major political crisis.
He had been forced to wear the mask because he could not risk being recognized.
"If I am seen here, my enemies will use it against me," he said.
A certain woman had some letters and a photograph that belonged to him.
She had threatened to send them to his family and to the public.
If she did this, his future would be completely destroyed.
He had tried to buy the papers from her, but she had refused his money.
Now, his only hope was that Holmes could find a way to get them back.
Holmes listened to this story with great attention, noting every small detail.
He asked several questions about the woman and where she kept the papers.
The gentleman gave us all the information he had, though some details were still unclear.
"This is indeed a difficult case," Holmes said after a long pause.
"But I am confident that a solution can be found."
If he had not been confident, the visitor might have lost all hope.
But because Holmes was so calm, the gentleman began to feel some hope.
He thanked Holmes and promised to pay any amount of money if the papers were returned.
After the gentleman had left, Holmes and Watson sat by the fire for a long time.
We discussed the different ways we could approach the problem.
If we did not act quickly, the papers might be made public very soon.
"I will need your help tomorrow, Watson," Holmes said to me.
I told him that I would be happy to do whatever was necessary.
If I had not visited him that evening, I would not have been part of this adventure.
I was glad that I had decided to go to Baker Street that night.
It was exciting to be working with my old friend once again.
The case proved to be one of the most difficult that Holmes had ever had.
It required all his skill and intelligence to find the papers.
If he had not been so brilliant, the secret would have been revealed.
But because he was able to think of things that others missed, he succeeded.
The papers were finally recovered, and the gentleman's future was saved.
It was an experience that I would never forget.
I learned a lot from watching Holmes work on this difficult case.
I was grateful for the opportunity to see my friend show his great ability once more.

quiz.q: What was the weather like when the narrator visited Sherlock Holmes?
What did the note warn Holmes to expect?
options: A visit from an old friend

quiz.q: What was unusual about the gentleman who visited Holmes?
options: He was wearing something over his face

sentences.text: It was a very cold winter evening.
The wind was strong and there was snow on the ground.
I decided to visit my old friend, Sherlock Holmes.
We had lived together for a long time.
Now, he was living alone in our old rooms on Baker Street.
I walked quickly through the dark streets.
I wanted to get out of the cold air.
Soon, I arrived at the house.
I went up the steps to his room.
It felt very familiar to me.
I knocked on the door and entered.
Holmes was sitting in his large chair near the fire.
The room was full of books and papers.
He looked very thin and tired.
His face was white, and he did not look healthy.
I felt sad to see my friend like this.
However, he was happy to see me.
When he started talking, his face changed completely.
I could see the old, bright energy in his eyes.
His mind was still very active.
He was ready for a new challenge.
He asked me about my life.
He wanted to know what I have done recently.
"I have been very busy with my work," I told him.
I told him about my work as a local doctor.
I had many patients to see every day.
I also told him about my family.
My life was very different now, but I was happy.
He listened to me carefully.
He was always interested in my news.
Then, Holmes started to talk about his own work.
He told me about his latest case.
It was a very difficult problem.
A rich man had lost something important.
The police did not know what to do.
"It is a strange case," Holmes said.
But I have some information.
If I follow these details, I will find the answer.
I am sure I can solve it soon.
He had great confidence in his work.
As we talked, I realized something important.
Holmes was still the same great expert.
He could notice tiny details that other people always missed.
For example, he looked at my shoes.
"You have walked through the cold park today," he said.
He was absolutely right.
I was amazed by his quick mind.
He always saw the truth immediately.
I was very happy to see my old friend again.
The room felt warm and comfortable.
We spent the rest of the evening talking about the past.
We remembered our old cases.
We laughed together about the funny things we had seen.
It was a wonderful night.
I had missed our long conversations.
It felt like the old days.
The next day, I was at my own home.
In the morning, I received a strange note.
The letter had no name on it.
I opened it and read the words carefully.
The note said that a gentleman would visit Holmes tonight.
He would arrive at a quarter to eight.
The writer of the note asked me to be there.
I wondered who this man could be.
The note did not say who the gentleman was.
It did not explain what he wanted from us.
However, it had a serious warning.
It warned Holmes to be very careful.
It said that the visitor was a man of high position.
"If you do not help him, there will be great trouble," the note said.
We had to expect something very important.
That night, I went back to Baker Street.
I wanted to see what would happen.
Holmes was waiting for me.
We sat together in the quiet room.
We watched the clock on the wall.
Finally, it was a quarter to eight.
At that exact moment, we heard a sound on the street.
A man walked up the steps.
He knocked on our door.
The gentleman had arrived, just as the note had said.
The door opened, and the man entered the room.
He was a very tall and thin man.
He was wearing expensive clothes, but he had a black cover over his face.
He did not want us to see his real face.
He spoke in a low, deep voice.
He looked at Holmes and then at me.
"I need your help," he said.
He told Holmes that he had a very serious problem.

quiz.q: What did the writer of the note warn Holmes about?
options: That he must be careful because the situation was very important.
That the police were looking for him.
That he should leave Baker Street immediately.

quiz.q: How did Watson know that Holmes's mind was still active?
options: Holmes told him he had decided to stop working.
Holmes noticed a small detail about Watson's shoes.
Holmes had already solved the visitor's problem.

quiz.q: What was unusual about the gentleman who arrived at Baker Street?
options: He had a black cover over his face.
He arrived much later than the note said.
